steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The notes service under /app is in rough shape: the C tree in src/ and www/ plus the root build recipe ought to produce a real notes_server binary at /app/notes_server, not a shell or Python stand-in, and canned HTTP responses will not pass. Verifiers talk to http://127.0.0.1:8080 and treat GET /api/notes as the health probe; see /app/docs/build_notes.md for the exact URLs they use and for urllib, signal, and quickjs harness imports used during checks.

For this first pass, focus on compilation, static assets, and a minimal list endpoint. Run make from /app so notes_server is a compiled ELF that listens on port 8080. Serve the browser files from /app/www so GET / returns the main HTML, /style.css returns the stylesheet, and /app.js returns the JavaScript. Unknown paths like /nonexistent.txt should be not found. If a request tries to escape the www tree with dot-dot segments, including percent-encoded forms, respond forbidden rather than opening anything outside that directory.

GET /api/notes should still answer with HTTP success and a JSON array, even when empty. Keep the main page wired for later work: the form id note-form, fields note-title and note-content, submit control btn-submit, and list container notes-list must stay on the page. Finish this pass before moving on to persistence and the browser script, and tell me when this pass is complete before you start the API work.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@10c43992d5dc:/app#

Analysis: I'm at the /app directory. I need to explore the current state of the project to understand what files exist, what the build system looks like, and what needs to be fixed.
Plan: First, I'll explore the directory structure, then look at the build notes documentation, existing source code, and Makefile to understand what needs to be done.

root@10c43992d5dc:/app# cat /app/docs/build_notes.md
# Build notes

The notes service links against libmicrohttpd and SQLite. Install the dev packages
for those libraries, then run `make` from `/app` to produce `notes_server`.

Web assets live under `/app/www` and are served from the HTTP root by URL path
(for example `/style.css` and `/app.js`).

Automated verifier traffic uses http://127.0.0.1:8080 as the base URL. Collection
health checks call http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/notes. Python verifier harnesses may
use urllib for HTTP, signal for subprocess timing, and quickjs when executing app.js.
root@10c43992d5dc:/app# cat /app/Makefile
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra
LDFLAGS = -lmicrohttpd -lsqlite3

SOURCES = src/main.c src/routes.c
TARGET = notes_server

$(TARGET): $(SOURCES)
        $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(SOURCES) $(LDFLAGS)

clean:
        rm -f $(TARGET)
root@10c43992d5dc:/app#

steps.message:
Analysis: I can see the project structure. The Makefile builds notes_server from src/main.c and src/routes.c, linking against libmicrohttpd and libsqlite3. I need to look at the existing source files and www files to understand what's already implemented.
Plan: Let me examine the existing source code files and www assets to understand what's there and what needs to be fixed/completed.

Analysis: I have a good picture of the project. The main.c looks fairly complete with static file serving and route handling. However:
1. The Makefile only compiles src/main.c and src/routes.c but the code references db.h functions (init_db, close_db, get_total_notes_count, get_notes_paginated_json)
2. I need to see db.c and db.h to understand what database functions exist
3. I need to install libmicrohttpd-dev and libsqlite3-dev
4. The routes.c output was truncated - I need to see the full file
5. The app.js points to port 9090 instead of 8080
Plan: Let me look at the remaining source files (db.c, db.h, auth.c, auth.h) and then fix compilation issues.

Analysis: Now I have a complete picture of the project. The issues are:
1. Need to install libmicrohttpd-dev and libsqlite3-dev
2. Makefile doesn't include src/db.c and lacks -Isrc for include paths
3. routes.c has a non-static request_completed function that will conflict with main.c's static one
4. The url_contains_encoded_dotdot only checks %2e%2e but not mixed forms like .%2e or %2e.
5. app.js references port 9090 instead of 8080

I need to fix all these and get it compiling.
Plan: 1. Install required dev packages
2. Fix the Makefile to include all source files and proper include paths
3. Fix the dot-dot detection to handle partial percent-encoding
4. Fix app.js to use port 8080
5. Handle the duplicate request_completed function in routes.c
6. Compile and test

steps.message:
Analysis: Good - the dev packages are already installed. Now I need to fix the code. Key issues:
1. Makefile doesn't include src/db.c
2. routes.c has a duplicate request_completed function that conflicts with main.c
3. app.js references port 9090 instead of 8080
4. The dot-dot detection needs to handle mixed percent-encoded forms like %2e. or .%2e

Let me see the full routes.c since it was truncated, then fix all issues.
Plan: Let me get the full routes.c with line numbers, then rewrite all necessary files to fix compilation and functionality.

Analysis: Now I have the full picture. Issues to fix:
1. Makefile needs to include src/db.c and add -Isrc to CFLAGS for header resolution
2. routes.c has an unused static request_completed that will trigger warnings (and main.c already has its own)
3. app.js uses port 9090 instead of 8080
4. The dot-dot detection in main.c should also handle mixed percent-encoded forms like `.%2e` or `%2e.`

Let me fix all these issues.
Plan: 1. Rewrite Makefile to include all source files and proper include path
2. Remove unused request_completed from routes.c
3. Fix app.js to use port 8080
4. Improve dot-dot detection in main.c to handle all variants
5. Build and test

Analysis: Excellent! The server is running and GET / returns the HTML correctly. Now let me test all the other required endpoints.
Plan: Test all endpoints: /style.css, /app.js, /api/notes, /nonexistent.txt (404), and path traversal attempts (403).

Analysis: The path traversal tests are returning 404 instead of 403. This is likely because curl normalizes the URL before sending (removing `..` segments). I need to test with `--path-as-is` flag to send the actual path without normalization.
Plan: Test with curl --path-as-is to ensure the dot-dot segments are actually sent to the server, and verify the 403 response.

- GET / returns HTML correctly (200)
- /style.css returns CSS (200)
- /app.js returns JavaScript (200)

- notes_server is a compiled ELF binary
Plan: Let me verify content-type headers are correct and also test uppercase percent-encoded variants to be thorough. Then I'll stop the server, do a clean rebuild to confirm everything works from scratch, and declare this pass complete.

steps.message:
Analysis: The Content-Type headers aren't showing up for static files when using HEAD (curl -I). This is because my code only handles method "GET" and HEAD falls through to 404. I need to either also handle HEAD or use a different approach. Let me verify that GET requests actually include Content-Type headers, and also fix HEAD support.
Plan: 1. Check if GET requests include Content-Type for static files (verifiers likely use GET)
2. Fix the code to also handle HEAD requests for static files
3. Rebuild and test

steps.message:
Analysis: GET requests properly return Content-Type headers. The HEAD issue was because curl -I sends a HEAD request which my code doesn't handle (only checks for "GET"). Libmicrohttpd doesn't automatically convert GET handlers to HEAD handlers - we need to explicitly handle HEAD as well. Let me fix this since verifiers might use HEAD requests too.
Plan: 1. Fix main.c to also handle HEAD method for static files
2. Also check that the HEAD request issue is actually a problem - let me first just verify all GET content types work
3. Rebuild and test

steps.message:
Analysis: All requirements for this first pass are verified:
1. ✅ `make` from /app produces notes_server as a compiled ELF 64-bit binary
2. ✅ Server listens on port 8080
3. ✅ GET / returns HTML with all required elements: form#note-form, input#note-title, textarea#note-content, button#btn-submit, div#notes-list
4. ✅ GET /style.css returns the stylesheet with text/css content-type
5. ✅ GET /app.js returns the JavaScript with application/javascript content-type
6. ✅ GET /nonexistent.txt returns 404 Not Found
7. ✅ Path traversal with .., %2e%2e, .%2e, %2e., %2E%2E all return 403 Forbidden
8. ✅ GET /api/notes returns HTTP 200 with JSON array []
9. ✅ Clean compilation with no errors or warnings
10. ✅ app.js uses relative /api/notes URL (fixed from port 9090)
Plan: The first pass is complete. All compilation, static asset serving, path traversal protection, and minimal API endpoint requirements are satisfied.
